concepts.title: Area between point of intersections
concepts.conceptBody:
Area bounded by two curves

 Here area will be 

$\begin{aligned} \text { Area } & \left.=\int_a^b \text { (right }- \text { left }\right) d y \\ & =\int_a^b[f(y)-g(y)] d y \end{aligned}$

see you can use any set of formula, but choose such a one which makes your calculation easier.

let us discuss some cases.

$\text { for such case both sets can be used i.e. }$ \begin{aligned} & \int (f(x)-g(x)) d x \\ & \int(f(y)-g(y)) d y \end{aligned}

examples.title: If area bounded by the curves $y^2=4 a x$ and $y=m x$ is $\frac{a^2}{3}$, then the value of $m$ is
